summary: Goshen Township held a special meeting work session on zoning, introducing Ben Yoder of Bricker and Eckler as new special counsel for zoning matters. Yoder presented an extensive training on the roles of the Zoning Commission, Board of Zoning Appeals (BZA), and Board of Trustees, covering hearing procedures, best practices for running meetings, maintaining good records, swearing in witnesses, the distinction between legislative and administrative actions, non-conforming uses, variances, conditional uses, and ethics considerations. The session brought together members of all three zoning-related boards for collaborative discussion.
action_items: Harry to send BZA/Zoning Commission chairperson hearing statements to Ben Yoder for review and critique
Ben Yoder to provide sign-in sheet format template to the boards
Harry to discuss specific use variance case (tractor-trailer repair shop in residential district) with Ben Yoder after the session
Trustees and boards to consider what Goshen Township should look like in the next 10 years
Trustee Hodges to pursue discussion on raising minimum house square footage above 1,400 sq ft
key_amounts: 1,400 square feet - current minimum house size requirement in Goshen Township
1,500 square feet - referenced as example updated minimum in non-conforming use discussion
150 attorneys at Bricker and Eckler firm
Over 1,000 political subdivisions represented by Bricker and Eckler
10,000 people - population threshold for limited home rule township exemption from regional planning commission
notable_topics: Introduction of Ben Yoder as special counsel for zoning
Roles and powers of Zoning Commission, BZA, and Board of Trustees
Difference between legislative and administrative actions in zoning
Hearing procedures and best practices for zoning meetings
Non-conforming uses and how to handle them
Swearing in witnesses and maintaining good records for legal defensibility
Role of Clermont County Regional Planning Commission
Application deadlines and staff report preparation
Raising minimum house square footage requirement
Zoning inspector decision appeals to BZA
attendees: Ben Yoder (Special Counsel, Bricker and Eckler)
notes: The transcript was truncated before the presentation concluded. The meeting was a collaborative training/workshop session rather than a decision-making meeting, bringing together all three zoning-related boards (Zoning Commission, BZA, and Trustees) for the first time. Roll call appeared to be conducted twice with different names - first calling Albus/Allen/Press, then Hodges/Allen/Hasselman, suggesting possible confusion between boards. Ben Yoder referenced his experience with a controversial mosque site plan review hearing in Hamilton Township (Warren County) as a teaching example. The township's zoning resolution was noted as relatively recent (2017) and considered to be of good quality.
title: Spc Mtg BOT 6.27.22 Zoning Workshop.mp4
